config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent Indonesia vs China native-or-English block. Each persona speaks only English or its own native language.
config.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: One-side-native cross cell: Indonesia persona speaks Indonesian; China persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas yang tidak boleh dikorbankan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena tanpa perlindungan hak, mayoritas bisa menekan yang lemah. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi harus dicapai dengan cara yang adil dan menghormati kebebasan dasar setiap orang. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun ketika hak individu dan kepentingan publik dijaga seimbang, bukan dengan menomorsatukan salah satunya secara mutlak.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not think society's interests should automatically take priority over the rights of the individual. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people emphasize collective stability, public order, and shared responsibility, because those values are deeply familiar and often important in real life. But I also think that if individual rights can be overridden too easily, then the "interest of society" becomes a vague excuse for abuse, and ordinary people end up with too little protection. A healthy society should balance the two, with clear limits on what the collective can demand from each person.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari pengalaman di Indonesia, saya melihat alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan, padahal yang paling rentan justru biasanya warga kecil yang suaranya paling lemah. Kepentingan publik memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh menjadi alasan untuk meniadakan kebebasan dasar, karena kalau hak individu mudah dikorbankan, masyarakat malah membuka pintu bagi penyalahgunaan kuasa. Menurut saya, yang benar adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan batas yang jelas, bukan memberi prioritas mutlak pada salah satunya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over individual rights as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people value social order and collective goals, because stability can matter a lot in daily life, but I also know that vague appeals to "the public interest" can easily be used to silence dissent or burden ordinary people. If individual rights are treated as secondary, then the state or the majority may decide too much for everyone, and that is dangerous even when the intention is good. A better principle is that society's interests matter, but only within firm limits that protect basic rights and prevent abuse.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus diprioritaskan di atas hak individu secara umum. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan warga kecil, padahal justru mereka yang paling sedikit punya kuasa untuk membela diri. Kepentingan sosial memang penting, tetapi kalau hak dasar mudah dikalahkan, yang terjadi bukan ketertiban yang sehat melainkan ruang besar untuk penyalahgunaan wewenang. Jadi, yang paling tepat menurut saya adalah menyeimbangkan keduanya dengan batas yang tegas, bukan memberi prioritas mutlak pada masyarakat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe the interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual as a general rule. From my perspective as someone from China, I understand why people stress order, harmony, and collective goals, because those values can genuinely help society function and can be very important in everyday life. But I also think that once "society" is treated as automatically more important, it becomes too easy for power to ignore ordinary people, especially when the language of the common good is used to justify pressure, censorship, or unfair treatment. For me, the right position is not to elevate the individual above society, but to insist that social interests must always be limited by basic rights.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
